search_queries_used: site:ebi.aomrc.org.uk tonsillectomy recurrent tonsillitis documented episodes disabling shared decision 2024
site:dha.gov.ae sore throat guideline recurrent tonsillitis red flags
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic Centor score, antibiotic, analgesic, steroid, test, procedure, or referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Episode counts and shared decision require verified records and clinician judgement; exclusions may apply.
No score, test, medicine, dose, treatment, procedure, referral, or threshold is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults and children with recurrent clinician-diagnosed acute tonsillitis; sleep-disordered breathing, suspected cancer, quinsy, emergency presentations, immune deficiency, and PFAPA require separate pathways.
setting_applicability: UAE primary, telehealth, paediatric, urgent, or ENT recurrent-tonsillitis assessment.
UAE_applicability: DHA gives direct UAE acute sore-throat context; EBI surgical thresholds and commissioning context require UAE ENT, payer, and surgical adaptation.
recency_verification: Current DHA Issue 2 and EBI guidance last reviewed in 2024 were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: DHA Issue 2 and the reviewed EBI page are current registered versions.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact EBI section supports episode history, onset and recurrence pattern, symptom and functional impact, associated tonsillitis features, relevant negatives, concerns, and clinician-performed ENT or general assessment without applying a procedural threshold.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting recurrent sore-throat episodes attributed by a clinician to acute tonsillitis, episode history, normal-function impact, work or school impact, and complications without applying an automatic procedural threshold.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact shared-decision and exclusions section supports clinician concern, entered plan, safety net, follow-up, and questions without recommending tonsillectomy or setting a threshold.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports recording a clinician-completed benefit-risk-alternative discussion and recognizing sleep-disordered breathing, suspected cancer, recurrent quinsy, emergency presentations, immune deficiency, and other specialist contexts without recommending surgery.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact DHA sore-throat features and testing section was reviewed for UAE acute-episode context, but no diagnostic score or automatic test interpretation was mapped.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists swallowing pain, fever, cough and URTI features, tonsillar findings, cervical nodes, rash, and clinician-selected GAS testing context.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact DHA red-flag section was reviewed for acute safety context; no automatic escalation or referral text was mapped.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists airway, swallowing, breathing, pain, immune compromise, neck stiffness, dehydration, and complication prompts for escalation.

unresolved_source_gaps: Age, verified episode count and dates, fever, nodes, exudate, testing, treatment, school or work loss, quinsy, sleep symptoms, immune disease, examination, diagnosis, surgery discussion, and referral remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
